purpose: Small synthetic controls showing that retrieval quality and synthesis quality can fail independently. These fixtures do not assert real-source truth.

fixtures.prompt: Use systematic-source-discovery. Triage this controlled synthetic candidate packet and separate central sources from decoys before any synthesis.
fixtures.expected_decision: Select central sources and reject decoys
required_uncertainties: No external database search has been run
allowed_claims: The packet can support a controlled candidate-source triage only.
disallowed_claims: private interview notes can be used
hard_fail_patterns: private interview notes can be used
retrieval_recall_notes: A passing retrieval answer keeps both central synthetic sources visible before any synthesis.
retrieval_precision_notes: A passing retrieval answer rejects marketing and news decoys as non-central evidence.

retrieval recall.3: Names one central synthetic source but misses another expected source.
retrieval recall.4: Names all expected central synthetic sources from the packet.
retrieval recall.5: Names all expected sources and explains why they are central before synthesis.

retrieval precision.3: Rejects at least one weak source but leaves another decoy ambiguous.
retrieval precision.4: Rejects every decoy and forbidden source as non-central evidence.
retrieval precision.5: Separates central, acceptable-context, decoy, and forbidden sources with concise reasons.

source quality discrimination.3: Distinguishes scholarly candidates from weak sources in general terms.
source quality discrimination.4: Uses packet-specific method, marketing, news, and privacy boundaries.
source quality discrimination.5: Explains source roles without converting source selection into source-truth claims.

citation/locator discipline.4: Avoids invented locators and states that no real database search was run.
citation/locator discipline.5: Keeps all source identifiers synthetic and requests logged retrieval before citation use.

fixtures.prompt: Use literature-review-mapper. Synthesize this controlled synthetic packet without flattening disagreement or claiming field consensus.
required_uncertainties: The packet does not resolve which interpretation is stronger.
allowed_claims: The packet supports a provisional synthesis with unresolved disagreement.
synthesis_required_claim_boundaries: The packet supports a provisional synthesis with unresolved disagreement.
synthesis_required_disagreements: Synthetic Source A reports coordination benefits while Synthetic Source B warns that dashboard metrics narrow debate.
synthesis_uncertainty_requirements: The packet does not resolve which interpretation is stronger.

synthesis faithfulness.3: Summarizes the packet but blurs at least one source boundary.
synthesis faithfulness.4: Preserves the packet's supported claim boundary without importing outside consensus.
synthesis faithfulness.5: Separates each source role and keeps every synthesis claim source-bound.

synthesis uncertainty.4: States that the packet does not resolve which interpretation is stronger.
synthesis uncertainty.5: Explains how unresolved sample and time-period differences limit the synthesis.

disagreement handling.3: Acknowledges disagreement but treats one side as default.
disagreement handling.4: Preserves the coordination-benefit and metric-critique disagreement.
disagreement handling.5: States both positions, their evidence limits, and the next evidence needed to adjudicate them.

citation/locator discipline.4: Keeps the case synthetic and does not invent locators or real bibliographic metadata.
citation/locator discipline.5: Requests source-level bibliographic verification before any citation or field-state claim.
